title: Hymenostegia longituba
taxonomy: Hymenostegia longituba belongs to the family Sapindaceae and is native to tropical Africa. It is a species of tree in the genus Hymenostegia, which is primarily found in the tropical regions of the continent. The Sapindaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants, including trees and shrubs, commonly known as soapberry or soapbark trees. This family is characterized by the presence of a unique type of fruit, often referred to as a drupe. Hymenostegia longituba is a relatively rare species within its genus and family.

The bark and roots of the plant are used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions.
